description: Promoted deterministic fixture from openai replay artifact. Source fixture: voucher-dropoff-w10-on. Replay created at: 2026-06-18T16:53:02.698Z.

anomaly.impact: Voucher payment value collapsed to about 5% of baseline and stayed depressed.

diagnosis.conclusion: Voucher payment volume collapsed to roughly 5% of baseline starting week 10 and remained depressed through the end of the data horizon. Credit card and boleto did not absorb the lost volume, and order count fell, so this is likely a program-level voucher change rather than clean substitution.
evidence: get_anomaly_context(metric=payment_value, dimension=payment_type, segment=voucher) returned pct_change=-0.95 for weeks 10-26.
Credit_card and boleto did not rise enough to absorb the lost voucher volume.
Total order_count was about 8% below baseline after the voucher cliff.
The drop was uniform across SP, RJ, and MG.

hypothesesConsidered.hypothesis: Voucher volume substituted to credit card or boleto
hypothesesConsidered.reasoning: Other payment types did not rise and total order count fell.
The cliff-shaped onset and sustained cross-state impact fit suspension, expiry, or restriction.

affectedCustomers.segmentDescription: Customers who previously used voucher payment in weeks 1-9

promotion.note: This fixture captures the final replay answer deterministically; it does not reconstruct the live provider tool loop.
